# Test basic mcpdiff functionality
go build -o mcpdiff .

# Create test MCP trace files
cat > test1.mcp << 'EOF'
# mcptrace:v1
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05"}}
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}}}
EOF

cat > test2.mcp << 'EOF'
# mcptrace:v1
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05"}}
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{"tools":{}}}}
EOF

# Test help output
! ./mcpdiff -h
stderr 'Usage.*mcpdiff'

# Test with no arguments
! ./mcpdiff
stderr 'Usage.*mcpdiff'

# Test diffing two files
./mcpdiff test1.mcp test2.mcp
stdout 'diff'

# Test with identical files
cp test1.mcp test3.mcp
./mcpdiff test1.mcp test3.mcp
! stdout 'diff'

# Test with non-existent file
! ./mcpdiff nonexistent.mcp test1.mcp
stderr 'error.*nonexistent'

# Clean up
rm -f mcpdiff test1.mcp test2.mcp test3.mcp
